parsed_review.summary: CP00-219 (RP06 DMS Core — closes RP06.P05 fixture-case tail M06–M10, opens RP06.P06 permission-matrix foundation M00–M02) is a descriptor-only, risk-class-C pack of 150 units that does NOT over-claim production readiness, and its closeout-readiness evidence holds up under CP178+ hardening. The pack-level claim is correctly bounded: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both report production_ready=false with status "implemented_pending_review" / "implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout"; the only positive signal is hermes production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && matrix.valid), which is explicitly a CANDIDATE, not a final/enterprise-trust claim. Authority boundary is respected throughout: service.js descriptors and validators.js assert claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Claude stays a read-only reviewer (read_only=true, allowed_tools strictly [Read,Grep,Glob]) and is never promoted to final approver. No-write / no-real-data boundaries verified: freezeCp219Result forces synthetic_only=true, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false and ~30 runtime booleans false on every result; grep of packages/dms/src found no filesystem writes, network/child_process calls, secrets, or PII. Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor-only review packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape "descriptor_only_p05_closeout_p06_permission_matrix_no_write"), not product runtime. Coverage is validated against the LIVE plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:413, deviation_from_plan=false) rather than a self-referential snapshot, and validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs (read-only; no write APIs) asserts the live-plan entry exists and reconciles unit_count, phase_counts {RP06.P05:90, RP06.P06:60}, deliverable_counts summing to 150, deny-over-allow/cross-wall boundaries, and the CP00-220 / RP06.P06.M02.S21 handoff. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness-report auth_preflight passed (exit 0, stdout 1432 bytes non-zero, valid JSON {"ok":true}); no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced-JSON-with-prose, tool-call-shaped output, or missing required fields are accepted as evidence (forbidden_review_evidence list inherited from CP198). Identity/coverage/distribution values reconcile across baseline, live plan, contract, registry binding, manifest, and command-evidence with no drift. One non-blocking P3 informational finding: the CP219 validator unit tests exercise only happy-path inputs, so the error-detection branches are not directly covered. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS — does not block pack closeout or goal closeout; final enterprise-trust closure remains gated on the required human approval, consistent with the stated authority boundary.

findings.title: CP219 validators are exercised only on happy-path inputs; error-detection branches are not unit-tested
findings.details: The three CP00-219 tests in model.test.js assert coverage validity, descriptor-only deny-over-allow/wall boundaries, and evidence-packet shape using only well-formed descriptors produced by the service layer. validateDmsCoreCp219Coverage and validateDmsCoreCp219P05CloseoutP06PermissionMatrixDescriptor contain substantial rejection logic (unit_count/first/last mismatch, duplicate units, non-RP06 phases, runtime_execution!=false, cross_wall_access_allowed!=false, missing human-approval route, contract drift, etc.), but no test feeds a tampered/mutated descriptor to confirm valid===false. The validators therefore have no direct negative-path coverage; a regression that weakened a guard could pass the suite undetected. This is informational only: the validators are read-only descriptor checks, the live validation script independently re-asserts the same invariants, and the pack does not claim production_ready, so this does not affect the correctness of the current closeout evidence.
findings.recommendation: Add at least one negative-path unit test per CP219 validator that clones a valid descriptor, mutates a single guarded field (e.g. set a row's runtime_execution=true or cross_wall_access_allowed=true, or change unit_count), and asserts the validator returns valid===false with the expected error. This locks in the rejection branches against future regressions. Non-blocking for CP00-219 closeout.
